CHANGELOG.html.title: Changelog
CHANGELOG.html.summary: Changelog 1.0.2 - 2026-03-06 Patch. Fix documentation links. Fix sample links. 1.0.1 - 2026-03-04 Initial release. Add core functionality. Add editor capabilities. Add documentation. Add samples.

api/TextFx.BaseModifier.html.title: Class BaseModifier
api/TextFx.BaseModifier.html.summary: Class BaseModifier Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll The base class of all modifiers responsible for applying an effect on a character. [Serializable] public abstract class BaseModifier Inheritance object BaseModifier Derived ColorModifier OpacityModifier PositionModifier RotationModifier ScaleModifier Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Enabled Is this modifier enabled? public bool Enabled { get; set; } Property Value bool Invert Should the effect be inverted? (Strength is flipped respecting the group mask). public bool Invert { get; set; } Property Value bool Methods Apply(ref ModificationContext) Updates the context values with the modifier applied. public abstract void Apply(ref ModificationContext context) Parameters context ModificationContext The context to update.

api/TextFx.ColorModifier.html.title: Class ColorModifier
api/TextFx.ColorModifier.html.summary: Class ColorModifier Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll Controls the color of characters. [Serializable] public class ColorModifier : BaseModifier Inheritance object BaseModifier ColorModifier Inherited Members BaseModifier.Enabled BaseModifier.Invert object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Color The color to apply. The final color is computed as a linear interpolation between the original color and this color based on the strength. public Color32 Color { get; set; } Property Value Color32 Methods Apply(ref ModificationContext) Updates the context values with the modifier applied. public override void Apply(ref ModificationContext context) Parameters context ModificationContext The context to update.

api/TextFx.Mask.html.title: Class Mask
api/TextFx.Mask.html.summary: Class Mask Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll A simple fractional mask that defines a range within the [0-1] space. Used to determine which characters should be affected by a group based on their position within the text. Values are fractional [0-1] and does not depend on the actual width. [Serializable] public class Mask Inheritance object Mask Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties End Indicates the end of the mask. The assigned value is clamped to the range [0,1]. public float End { get; set; } Property Value float Start Indicates the start of the mask. The assigned value is clamped to the range [0,1]. public float Start { get; set; } Property Value float

api/TextFx.ModificationContext.html.title: Struct ModificationContext
api/TextFx.ModificationContext.html.summary: Struct ModificationContext Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll Context used internally to compute and pass modified character information across all groups and their modifiers sequentially. Each modifier applies its properties to this context, which accumulates the final transformation, color, and opacity for each character. public struct ModificationContext Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Properties Color The color computed so far. public Color32 Color { readonly get; set; } Property Value Color32 Opacity The opacity computed so far. public float Opacity { readonly get; set; } Property Value float Pivot The pivot that should be used for rotation and scaling effects. public Vector3 Pivot { readonly get; set; } Property Value Vector3 Strength The strength multiplier for the current modifier effect [0-1]. public float Strength { readonly get; set; } Property Value float Transformation The transformation computed so far. public Matrix4x4 Transformation { readonly get; set; } Property Value Matrix4x4

api/TextFx.ModifierGroup.html.title: Class ModifierGroup
api/TextFx.ModifierGroup.html.summary: Class ModifierGroup Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll A group encapsulating characters and provides an interface to control various modifiers and settings related to the characters in the group. [Serializable] public class ModifierGroup Inheritance object ModifierGroup Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties ColorModifier Controls the color of characters. public ColorModifier ColorModifier { get; } Property Value ColorModifier Curve The curve that defines the strength of the effects over the range. public PlateauCurve Curve { get; } Property Value PlateauCurve Extent The fractional extent of the range starting at offset that is affected by the effects. Value of 1 corresponds to the sum of all the widths of the contributing character bounds. The assigned value must be non-negative. If the assigned value is negative, it will be transformed to 0. public float Extent { get; set; } Property Value float Mask Fractional mask controlling the characters affected in this group. public Mask Mask { get; } Property Value Mask Offset The fractional offset of the group. The offset is the starting fractional position [0-1] of the range affected by the effects. Value of 0 corresponds to the left bound of the first contributing character. Value of1 corresponds to the right bound of the last contributing character. Paired with extent, it defines the range for which the group modifiers are applied. public float Offset { get; set; } Property Value float OpacityModifier Controls the opacity of characters. public OpacityModifier OpacityModifier { get; } Property Value OpacityModifier OrderedModifiers The order in which the modifiers should be applied to make the transformations intuitive. public IReadOnlyList<BaseModifier> OrderedModifiers { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<BaseModifier> Pivot Shifts the pivot of the chatacters affected by the group. Default pivot lies on the baseline at the center of each character. public Vector3 Pivot { get; set; } Property Value Vector3 PositionModifier Controls the local translation of characters. public PositionModifier PositionModifier { get; } Property Value PositionModifier RotationModifier Controls the local rotation of characters around the configured pivot. public RotationModifier RotationModifier { get; } Property Value RotationModifier ScaleModifier Controls the local scale of characters around the configured pivot. public ScaleModifier ScaleModifier { get; } Property Value ScaleModifier Strength The strength multiplier for the group. The value must be in range [0,1]. The assigned value is clamped to this range. public float Strength { get; set; } Property Value float

api/TextFx.OpacityModifier.html.title: Class OpacityModifier
api/TextFx.OpacityModifier.html.summary: Class OpacityModifier Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll Controls the opacity of characters. [Serializable] public class OpacityModifier : BaseModifier Inheritance object BaseModifier OpacityModifier Inherited Members BaseModifier.Enabled BaseModifier.Invert object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Opacity The opacity to apply. The value must be in range [0,1]. The assigned value is clamped to this range. public float Opacity { get; set; } Property Value float Methods Apply(ref ModificationContext) Updates the context values with the modifier applied. public override void Apply(ref ModificationContext context) Parameters context ModificationContext The context to update.

api/TextFx.PlateauCurve.html.title: Class PlateauCurve
api/TextFx.PlateauCurve.html.summary: Class PlateauCurve Namespace TextFx Assembly TextFx.dll A special type of curve that starts rising from 0 to 1 following a profile (Rising profile), is 1 for a certain range, and then falls back to 0 following another profile (Falling profile). The values used are fractional i.e, 0 is considered the starting point and 1, the ending point no matter the actual width of the range it is applied to. [Serializable] public class PlateauCurve Inheritance object PlateauCurve Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Fall Fractional value [0-1] indicating when the curve should start falling from 1 to reach 0. The assigned value is clamped to the range [0,1]. public float Fall { get; set; } Property Value float FallingProfile Smoothly transitions the fall using the curve. Evaluated right to left (x = 1 to x = 0). Note: Only used if separateProfiles is true. public AnimationCurve FallingProfile { get; set; } Property Value AnimationCurve Rise Fractional value [0-1] indicating when the curve should reach 1 starting from 0. The assigned value is clamped to the range [0,1]. public float Rise { get; set; } Property Value float RisingProfile Smoothly trasitions the rise using the curve. Note: If separate profiles is disabled, this will be used as the falling profile as well where falling profile is evaluated right to left (x = 1 to x = 0). public AnimationCurve RisingProfile { get; set; } Property Value AnimationCurve SeparateProfiles Should the curve use separate rising and falling profiles? public bool SeparateProfiles { get; set; } Property Value bool Methods Evaluate(float) Evaluates the curve at a given time. public float Evaluate(float time) Parameters time float The time to evaluate the curve at. Returns float The value of the curve at the given time.

When enabled, the text mesh updates every frame. Disable it and call UpdateMesh() only on changes for performance. Effective only during play mode. public bool AutoUpdate { get; set; } Property Value bool Offset The global fractional offset that is added to all the group offsets. Offset is the starting fractional position of the range affected by the effects. Paired with extent of groups, it defines the range for which the group modifiers are applied. public float Offset { get; set; } Property Value float RightToLeft Enable right to left mode? public bool RightToLeft { get; set; } Property Value bool Strength The global strength multiplier for all the effects. The value must be in range [0,1]. This method is only effective when auto update is disabled and the application is playing. public void UpdateMesh()

api/TextFx.html.title: Namespace TextFx
api/TextFx.html.summary: Namespace TextFx Classes BaseModifier The base class of all modifiers responsible for applying an effect on a character. ColorModifier Controls the color of characters. Mask A simple fractional mask that defines a range within the [0-1] space. Used to determine which characters should be affected by a group based on their position within the text. Values are fractional [0-1] and does not depend on the actual width. ModifierGroup A group encapsulating characters and provides an interface to control various modifiers and settings related to the characters in the group. OpacityModifier Controls the opacity of characters. PlateauCurve A special type of curve that starts rising from 0 to 1 following a profile (Rising profile), is 1 for a certain range, and then falls back to 0 following another profile (Falling profile). The values used are fractional i.e, 0 is considered the starting point and 1, the ending point no matter the actual width of the range it is applied to. PositionModifier Controls the local translation of characters. RotationModifier Controls the local rotation of characters around the configured pivot. ScaleModifier Controls the local scale of characters around the configured pivot. TextFxController The main TextFx controller provides an interface to create text effects using modifier groups and exposes controls to drive animations. This controller provides functionalities that support any TMPro.TMP_Text implementations. TextFxUtils Structs ModificationContext Context used internally to compute and pass modified character information across all groups and their modifiers sequentially. Each modifier applies its properties to this context, which accumulates the final transformation, color, and opacity for each character.

manual/concepts/masks.html.title: Masks
manual/concepts/masks.html.summary: Masks Masks let you control the characters affected by a group. By combining multiple groups with different masks, you can precisely control the overall behaviour of a sentence, a word or even a single character. Properties The following section provides a description of each property associated with masks. Start Determines where the mask begins. The value must be in range [0,1]. End Determines where the mask stops. The value must be in range [0,1]. Warning There may be unpredictable behaviour if start exceeds end.

manual/concepts/modifier-groups.html.title: Modifier Groups
manual/concepts/modifier-groups.html.summary: Modifier Groups Modifier groups are the building blocks of effects. They encapsulate a collection of modifiers and define how effects are applied to specific ranges of the text. They are additive i.e., the effects of the overlapping groups are added up. Properties The following section provides a comprehensive description of each property associated with modifier groups. Strength Controls the overall intensity of all effects in the group. The value is in range [0,1]. Offset Offset controls the starting point of the effect range. The effect range defines how strength is applied on each character. Paired with the curve property, you can define how the effects transition when sliding the range. Extent Defines how much of the text the group affects as a fractional width. The effect range spans from Offset to Offset + Extent. Pivot Shifts the transformation pivot for rotation and scaling effects for a group. The default pivot is at each character's baseline, and the pivot shift is applied to all characters in the group. Tip When a group is selected, the character bounds and the pivot positions are drawn in the scene view. Curve Defines how effect strength varies across the group's effect range using a plateau curve. The curve controls the transition from no effect to full effect and back, allowing for smooth fade-in and fade-out zones. Check plateau curve for an extensive overview. Mask The mask defines a range that limits which characters are affected. The value is fractional and is in range [0,1]. Check Masks for an extensive overview. Modifiers The list of modifiers provides various transformation and visual effects that can be applied to characters within the group's range. Each modifier group contains built-in modifiers that are applied in a specific order for predictable results. See Modifiers for more information.

manual/concepts/modifiers.html.title: Modifiers
manual/concepts/modifiers.html.summary: Modifiers Modifiers determine how a character is modified based on the other settings of the group. A group has multiple modifiers which you can enable individually. Types The following section describes the available modifier types. Position Modifier Controls the local position of the characters. Rotation Modifier Controls the local rotation of characters with the configured pivot of the group. Scale Modifier Controls the local scale of characters with the configured pivot of the group. Color Modifier Controls the color of the characters. Opacity Modifier Controls the opacity of the characters. Note The magnitude of each of these properties are determined using the strength and the effect range of the group.

manual/concepts/plateau-curve.html.title: Plateau Curve
manual/concepts/plateau-curve.html.summary: Plateau Curve Plateau curve defines how effect strength varies across a modifier group's range. It controls the transition from no effect to full effect and back (shaped like a plateau), allowing for smooth fade-in and fade-out zones within the group's effect window. Properties The following section provides a description of each property associated with plateau curves. Rise Defines the point where the curve reaches maximum strength as a fractional position from 0 to 1 based on the size of the effect window. This determines how quickly the effect transitions from zero to full strength at the beginning of the range. Fall Defines the point where the curve starts falling from maximum strength as a fractional position from 0 to 1 based on the size of the effect window. This determines where the effect begins transitioning from full strength back to zero at the end of the range. Separate profiles Enables the use of separate animation curves for rising and falling transitions. When disabled, a single curve profile is used for both transitions. Rising profile The animation curve used for the rise transition when Separate Profiles is enabled. This curve controls the shape of the transition from zero to full effect strength. Falling profile The animation curve used for the fall transition when Separate Profiles is enabled. This curve controls the shape of the transition from full effect strength back to zero. Note that the x value used to evaluate the curve goes from x = 1 to x = 0 (Right to Left) for falling profiles. This is done intentionally to have a similar behaviour when using a single profile to control both rise and fall. Tip Even though the rising and falling profiles must adhere to y = 0 when x = 0 and y = 1 when x = 1 for a rising and falling transition for the effect range, it is not a hard rule and the warning can be ignored to achieve certain effects.

manual/concepts/textfx-controller.html.title: TextFx Controller
manual/concepts/textfx-controller.html.summary: TextFx Controller TextFxController is the main component of the system. It exposes various parameters that can be used to control the behaviour of a text. Properties The following section provides a comprehensive description of each property associated with TextFxController. Enabled The controller only applies effects while the component is enabled. Disabling the component removes all applied effects. Auto Update When enabled, the text mesh updates automatically every frame. If your controller's values aren't changing between frames, this can be inefficient. Disabling this option requires you to manually call TextFxController.UpdateMesh in play mode whenever you modify the controller, giving you precise control over mesh updates and better performance optimization. Outside play mode, this setting is ignored. Right To Left Enables Right To Left mode. In this mode, all properties work in right-to-left orientation without changing their behavior. Multiple lines are also supported. Note When Right To Left is enabled, calculations for every line starts from the right hand side. This means that an offset of 0 starts at the right hand side instead of the left for every line, extent grows towards left, curves rise from right, etc. Strength The global strength of all modifier groups applied to the text. This property provides a single control to adjust the intensity of all modifier groups. The strength is a fractional value in range [0,1]. Offset Adds a global offset to all the local offsets of the modifier groups. Offset controls the starting point of the effect range of each group. Modifier Groups The list of modifier groups that provide several modifiers and settings to control the range of characters affected. Modifier groups are additive i.e., the effects sum up for overlapping groups. Check Modifier Groups for more information.

manual/getting-started/installation.html.title: Installation
manual/getting-started/installation.html.summary: Installation This section outlines the installation process of TextFx. Prerequisites TextFx requires TextMeshPro which comes from com.unity.ugui package. Ensure TextMeshPro essentials are imported in your game. Install via OpenUPM (Recommended) To include TextFx in your game via OpenUPM, follow these steps: Setup your project to work with the OpenUPM CLI. Follow the docs OpenUPM Docs. To add the latest version of TextFx using the OpenUPM CLI, execute the command: openupm add me.freedee.text-fx in the root directory of your game. Open your game window in Unity. TextFx will be available to use in your project. Install via Git URL To include TextFx in your game via Git URL, follow these steps: Open the Package Manager in Unity via Window > Package Manager. Click the + button in the top-left corner and select Add package from git URL.... Enter the following URL, replacing v1.0.1 with your desired version: https://github.com/kay-af/TextFx.git#v1.0.1-git. Click Add and wait for Unity to download and install the package. Install Manually To install TextFx manually, follow these steps: Download the latest release from the GitHub Releases page. Extract the downloaded archive. Copy the me.freedee.text-fx folder into the Packages folder of your Unity project. Open your project in Unity. TextFx will be available to use in your project.

manual/getting-started/overview.html.title: Overview
manual/getting-started/overview.html.summary: Overview Welcome to the TextFx documentation. Introduction TextFx is an open-source Unity package for animating texts, inspired by Rive's text animation system. It is designed to work with TextMeshPro and provides a structured way to rig character properties such as position, rotation, scale, and more. TextFx offers a robust programmatic API, allowing you to use your favorite tweening library to create beautiful text animations. Features overview Non-Destructive Workflow – Rigs are constructed using layered modifier groups that are composable and non-destructive in nature. Control - Precise control over which characters to target in the text and how they behave. Editor-friendly - Editor support for visualizing the controls in the scene view. Multi-Line Support - Multiple lines with different text alignments are supported. RTL Support - Fully compatible with right-to-left languages. Programmatic API – Includes a programmatic API that allows full runtime configuration and simplifies the creation of custom tools. Working Rigs in TextFx are configured using modifier groups. Each group controls how a particular property of the text changes with fine grained control over the range of characters affected. Multiple groups can be combined to create more complex effects. This makes it possible to build anything from simple fades and typewriter-style effects to layered animations that affect characters, words, or lines differently. Note TextFx itself does not provide an API for tweening parameters. It simply provides a way to rig your texts. It is recommended to use a library such as DoTween to drive the controller values. You can also use coroutines for simple animations.

manual/getting-started/usage.html.title: Usage
manual/getting-started/usage.html.summary: Usage Learn the basics of working with TextFx. Quickstart The main component of TextFx is the TextFxController. Add it to an existing TextMeshPro or TextMeshProUGUI game object to enable dynamic effects on the text. To add the TextFxController, select the game object and add the component via the inspector. Add Component > TextFx > TextFxController. Configuring Modifiers A group encapsulates a set of properties that affect a range of characters. To add a new group, click the + button under the modifier groups list in the inspector. Keep the default settings, scroll down to the modifiers section inside the group, and enable Position Modifier. The position modifier adjusts the relative position of characters. Set y = 15 to see the middle of the text rise. Experiment by changing the offset of the group to observe how the characters move. When a group is selected, helpers are drawn in the scene view to guide you in editing the group's parameters. Next, enable the Rotation modifier and set z = 30. Notice how characters within the group’s range are rotated. Play around with different settings and modifiers to see how the text responds. Check the Concepts section for a detailed explanation of all the available settings. Animating Once the text is rigged with the controller, you can drive the controller’s values via script to animate the text. Using a tweening library such as DoTween is recommended. For this example, we will use coroutines to drive the offset value and see how the text reacts to the change. Note When the auto update is enabled, the text updates every frame while the TextFxController component is enabled. For optimization, disable the controller when the text is static or after the animation completes. This removes the effects and stops updates. You can also turn off auto update and call the TextFxController.UpdateMesh method whenever you change any params.

manual/limitations.html.title: Limitations
manual/limitations.html.summary: Limitations There are few technical limitations of the system listed below. Compatibility with Unity's animation system Unity's built-in animation system doesn't support arrays, which is a significant limitation of the engine. For animating controller values, tweening library is recommended. Advanced effects Shader-based modifiers are not supported. Applying shader-based effects that modify character properties may interfere with or break the controller's effects.

manual/technical-details.html.title: Technical details
manual/technical-details.html.summary: Technical details This section covers some details of the system which may help you understand how things work under the hood. Contributing characters Whitespace characters do not contribute to the effect (You may have noticed the effect range snapping from character to character while editing). All the other characters are called Contributing characters. Each contributing character is assigned an extent of 1 / (Number of contributing characters). All the fractional calculations in the system account for this factor. For example, an offset has to travel the same amount of length for each character regardless of what their actual width is. This makes sure that the sliding effects have consistent timings for all contributing characters. Using the fact that each contributing character takes up 1 / (Number of contributing characters) extent, you can set modifier groups dynamically in the runtime targeting different groups. Some helper methods are provided in TextFxUtils class to do these calculations. You may refer to the scripting API of the same for more information. Character bounds When you select a modifier group in the editor, character bounds become visible and serve as guides for positioning the group's pivots. Note that italic text causes these bounds to be skewed for each character. TextFx performs all calculations based on the bottom edge of each character—specifically, the midpoint of the bottom border serves as the reference point for determining effect strength and other character-specific properties. Pivots By default, scale and rotation pivots are positioned at the horizontal midpoint of each character's bottom edge and aligned vertically with the baseline. You can adjust these pivot positions through the group's settings.
